blocks.content: Shirone led his parents to the reception room.
Having cried his eyes out since the front gate, the three of them walked the hallway with puffy eyes.
It would take time to compose themselves.
Whether the meeting would be prolonged or not was up to Clump's consideration, but Shirone would have plenty of time to reunite with his parents in the reception room.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Let me see, my son. Your face has weathered much since I last saw you. You've suffered. Truly suffered.
Tears welled up in Vincent's eyes again.
Though Shirone looked healthier than before, to his parents, it only seemed like he hadn't eaten or slept properly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I haven't suffered. I had fun reading books and passing the time. How have you been, Father?
Of course! I ate well and lived well.
Look how plump your mother has gotten.
We almost had another child because she became so pretty. Hahaha!

blocks.speaker: Vincent's mother
blocks.content: Dear, what nonsense are you spouting in front of our son?
After a year and six months apart, his parents looked much thinner.
No doubt they had worried about him every day, unable to eat properly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The thought made his heart ache again.
At first, he had been frantic, thinking something had happened to his child, but seeing Shirone so composed, it didn't seem like anything bad had occurred.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: So, why did you call us here? If your work is done, you could have just come home. Don't tell me they're trying to keep you here against your will?
